feedback: Strong post. Personal moment lands (Claude Code daily + 'I didn't. I picked it because it ships the best code.'). The two-events-as-one-signal framing is doing real analytical work, not just stacking news. Ends without a neat bow. The slight character overage (~1550-1580) is worth watching but clears the 2000-char fail threshold easily.

dimension_notes.voice: pass — multiple first-person refs, fragments present ('Sole-source mandate.', 'Same week. Stock down 25%.'), good burstiness, no banned words or em dashes
dimension_notes.framing: pass — two events explicitly bridged as one signal, not stacked as separate findings; genuine personal moment with Claude Code
dimension_notes.platform: pass — slightly over 1500-char sweet spot (~1560 est.) but well under 2000 fail threshold; hook works in first 210 chars; question is specific
dimension_notes.epistemic: pass — facts stated as facts, personal uncertainty stated as personal ('I don't have a clean answer'), no generic hedges
dimension_notes.structure: pass — asymmetric paragraphs, burstiness throughout, no neat-bow closing
dimension_notes.framework: pass — clear nut-graf structure: hook → facts → synthesis claim → personal stake → audience framing → uncertainty → question
